reading_paths.title: New to ShardLoom
reading_paths.summary: Start with the technical-preview posture, Vortex-first model, no-fallback contract, and claim gates.
reading_paths.claim_boundary: Orientation content explains current posture only and does not imply production or package-release readiness.

reading_paths.title: Run a local workflow
reading_paths.summary: Follow the local smoke and certified-workload path before reading deeper architecture docs.
reading_paths.claim_boundary: Local workflow recipes remain scoped technical-preview evidence, not production ETL or object-store support.

reading_paths.title: Understand benchmarks
reading_paths.summary: Read the benchmark page as evidence attribution, not a speed leaderboard or public ranking.
reading_paths.claim_boundary: Benchmark reading paths do not create performance, superiority, or Spark-displacement claims.

reading_paths.title: Understand Vortex-native paths
reading_paths.summary: Trace how prepared/native Vortex rows use source-backed scans, certificates, materialization boundaries, and provider evidence.
reading_paths.claim_boundary: Vortex-native explanations are scoped by artifact, operator, evidence, and claim gates.

reading_paths.title: Use Python, SQL, or DataFrame surfaces
reading_paths.summary: Separate ready-local Python wrapper use from report-only SQL/DataFrame planner and API posture.
reading_paths.claim_boundary: Python ergonomics and report-only SQL/DataFrame diagnostics do not imply broad SQL/DataFrame runtime support.

reading_paths.title: Know what is blocked
reading_paths.summary: Use blocker and boundary dossiers to avoid mistaking planned or report-only surfaces for runtime support.
reading_paths.claim_boundary: Blocked reading paths make unsupported surfaces visible and do not hide fallback execution.

reading_paths.title: Foundry and platform context
reading_paths.summary: Review Foundry, REST/control-plane, MCP/agent, package, and scale boundaries without inferring production platform support.
reading_paths.claim_boundary: Platform context is boundary documentation only; no Foundry, distributed, object-store, or managed-platform claim is created.

entries.title: What Is ShardLoom?
entries.summary: ShardLoom is a pre-release Vortex-first local compute engine foundation that exposes execution evidence instead of hiding unsupported work.
entries.claim_boundary: ShardLoom is a technical-preview local compute foundation, not a production platform or Spark-displacement claim.

entries.title: Vortex-First
entries.summary: Vortex-first means ShardLoom treats Vortex layouts as the native substrate for scoped local execution and evidence.
entries.claim_boundary: Vortex-first does not imply every Vortex layout, object-store source, or downstream sink is runtime-supported.

entries.title: No Fallback
entries.summary: ShardLoom must not silently delegate unsupported execution to another query engine.
entries.claim_boundary: No-fallback evidence is workload-scoped and does not create a broad production platform claim.

entries.title: Evidence-Certified Compute
entries.summary: Evidence-certified compute means a supported run records what executed, what materialized, what stayed native, and which claims remain closed.
entries.claim_boundary: Evidence certification is scoped to the workload and evidence refs that emitted it.

entries.title: Technical Preview
entries.summary: Technical preview is the public posture for ShardLoom while package publication, production APIs, broad SQL/DataFrame runtime, and scale claims remain gated.
entries.claim_boundary: Technical preview does not authorize production, performance, package-release, or platform claims.

entries.title: Execution Modes
entries.summary: Execution modes identify the source and preparation lane that a run used.
entries.claim_boundary: Execution mode labels are explanatory and must not be read as performance claims.

entries.title: Compatibility Import Certified
entries.summary: The compatibility lane imports local compatibility data into Vortex evidence paths and records certification costs.
entries.claim_boundary: Certification-lane evidence is not public speed, superiority, or best-default proof.

entries.title: Prepared Vortex
entries.summary: Prepared Vortex is the current runtime-development lane for already prepared Vortex artifacts.
entries.claim_boundary: Prepared Vortex is the main optimization direction, but current rows remain claim-gated.

entries.title: Native Vortex
entries.summary: Native Vortex is the lane for scoped execution over existing Vortex artifacts.
entries.claim_boundary: Native Vortex evidence is scoped to the supported artifacts and operators present in the repo.

entries.title: Direct Compatibility Transient
entries.summary: Direct compatibility transient is the planned lane for local compatibility data that does not first persist through Vortex.
entries.claim_boundary: Current direct transient posture is vocabulary/diagnostic only unless a future scoped runtime slice admits it.

entries.title: Auto Execution Mode
entries.summary: Auto mode is a transparent admission and selection path, not a hidden global fast-mode toggle.
entries.claim_boundary: Auto mode cannot upgrade support, evidence level, or claim status without explicit evidence.

entries.title: Engine Modes
entries.summary: Engine modes describe ShardLoom's internal batch, live, hybrid, or auto execution fabric posture.
entries.claim_boundary: Engine-mode vocabulary does not imply production live, hybrid, streaming, or distributed runtime support.

entries.title: Batch Engine
entries.summary: The batch engine is the finite, snapshot-oriented ShardLoom execution mode currently used by local smoke and prepared/native rows.
entries.claim_boundary: Batch evidence is scoped to local technical-preview workloads.

entries.title: Live Engine
entries.summary: The live engine is the planned continuous native execution posture for unbounded or change-oriented inputs.
entries.claim_boundary: Current live posture is not production streaming support.

entries.title: Hybrid Engine
entries.summary: The hybrid engine is the planned posture for cold Vortex data plus warm or hot delta/state overlays.
entries.claim_boundary: Hybrid vocabulary does not claim production continuous view or managed-platform runtime support.

entries.title: Fixture Smoke
entries.summary: Fixture smoke means a narrow local or generated fixture proves a contract without broad support claims.
entries.claim_boundary: Fixture smoke does not imply production or generalized runtime support.

entries.title: Vortex-Native
entries.summary: Vortex-native means ShardLoom treats Vortex representation as the native execution and persistence target where scoped evidence admits it.
entries.claim_boundary: Vortex-native is scoped by artifact, operator, source/sink, and evidence.

entries.title: Prepared Vortex Artifact
entries.summary: A prepared Vortex artifact is the reusable Vortex representation used to separate preparation from query/runtime timing.
entries.claim_boundary: Prepared artifacts are evidence-bearing state, not a performance claim by themselves.

entries.title: Source-Backed Scan
entries.summary: Source-backed scan evidence reports how a prepared/native row scanned source-backed Vortex data and what pushdown/materialization posture applied.
entries.claim_boundary: Source-backed scan evidence does not imply encoded-native or production scan support.

entries.title: Vortex Scan API
entries.summary: The Vortex Scan API is the upstream scan/source concept ShardLoom maps into scoped prepared/native pushdown evidence or deterministic blockers.
entries.claim_boundary: Scan API alignment does not authorize unsupported expressions or external fallback execution.

entries.title: Encoded Predicate Provider
entries.summary: Encoded predicate provider evidence records when a scoped selective-filter row can use encoded filter-column inputs without claiming full encoded-native execution.
entries.claim_boundary: Encoded predicate evidence is scoped and not an encoded-native operator claim.

entries.title: Selection Vector
entries.summary: A selection vector records selected row positions from an admitted predicate path so later work can avoid unnecessary row materialization.
entries.claim_boundary: Selection-vector use remains scoped until correctness, materialization, and claim-gate evidence supports broader use.

entries.title: Residual-Native
entries.summary: Residual-native means ShardLoom performs remaining operator work itself after admitted pushdown, without using an external engine fallback.
entries.claim_boundary: Residual-native is ShardLoom-native execution, but not necessarily encoded-native or optimized.

entries.title: Encoded-Native
entries.summary: Encoded-native means an operator executes directly over encoded representation with sufficient correctness and materialization evidence.
entries.claim_boundary: Most current rows keep encoded-native claims false unless end-to-end evidence proves the scoped path.

entries.title: Native I/O Certificate
entries.summary: A Native I/O certificate records whether a scoped run used the approved ShardLoom/Vortex-native I/O path.
entries.claim_boundary: Native I/O certificates are scoped to the workload and source/sink path that emitted them.

entries.title: Execution Certificate
entries.summary: An execution certificate is the structured record that ties an admitted run to execution, policy, and claim-gate evidence.
entries.claim_boundary: An execution certificate only proves the scoped run and attached evidence refs.

entries.title: Materialization Boundary
entries.summary: A materialization boundary records where data was decoded, materialized, converted, or kept native.
entries.claim_boundary: Boundary fields explain execution posture; they are not performance claims.

entries.title: Result-Sink Replay
entries.summary: Result-sink replay is the heavier proof path that writes, reopens, and verifies output evidence when full replay is requested.
entries.claim_boundary: Replay proof increases evidence depth but does not create a performance or production sink claim.

entries.title: Claim Gates
entries.summary: Claim gates state whether a row is claim-grade, fixture-smoke, report-only, blocked, unsupported, or not claim-grade.
entries.claim_boundary: If evidence is missing, public claims stay closed.

entries.title: No-Fallback Evidence
entries.summary: No-fallback evidence keeps fallback and external-engine invocation fields visible on capability, benchmark, and certificate rows.
entries.claim_boundary: No-fallback fields must not be used to imply unsupported surfaces are implemented.

entries.title: Report-Only
entries.summary: Report-only means ShardLoom can describe, classify, or diagnose a surface without executing it.
entries.claim_boundary: Report-only rows are intentionally not runtime support.

entries.title: Blocked Status
entries.summary: Blocked means a surface must fail closed until required policy, evidence, dependency, or runtime gates are satisfied.
entries.claim_boundary: Blocked rows are visible so users do not infer hidden support.

entries.title: Unsupported Diagnostics
entries.summary: Unsupported diagnostics are deterministic reports for paths ShardLoom does not currently execute.
entries.claim_boundary: Unsupported/report-only explanations reduce overclaiming risk but do not create new runtime support.

entries.title: External Baseline Only
entries.summary: External-baseline-only means other engines may provide comparison context or correctness oracles but never ShardLoom fallback execution.
entries.claim_boundary: External baselines cannot satisfy ShardLoom-native evidence gates.

entries.title: Benchmark Telemetry
entries.summary: Benchmark telemetry is evidence and attribution, not a speed leaderboard.
entries.claim_boundary: External engines are baseline context only and never fallback execution.

entries.title: Local Timing Context
entries.summary: Local timing context decomposes local benchmark stages so compatibility import, preparation, scan, compute, sink, and evidence costs stay visible.
entries.claim_boundary: Local timing context is not public ranking or superiority evidence.

entries.title: Prepared/Native Batch Smoke
entries.summary: Prepared/native batch smoke records single-process scoped rows for prepared Vortex and native Vortex development paths.
entries.claim_boundary: Batch smoke is structural evidence, not a hidden fast mode or performance claim.

entries.title: Source-State Reuse
entries.summary: Source-state reuse records whether source discovery, schema, parse, or operator state can be reused across scoped benchmark work.
entries.claim_boundary: Reuse evidence does not upgrade claim status or imply Vortex-native execution by itself.

entries.title: Benchmark Artifact Manifest
entries.summary: The benchmark artifact manifest records expected lanes, available lanes, missing lanes, environment, versions, and claim boundaries for static publishing.
entries.claim_boundary: A manifest makes missing baselines visible but does not make a performance claim.

entries.title: Benchmark Profile
entries.summary: Benchmark profiles separate smoke, full-local, Spark-context, extended, GPU, object-store, and fanout evidence lanes.
entries.claim_boundary: Profiles prevent mixed CPU/GPU/object-store/local interpretations and do not authorize claims.

entries.title: First 10 Minutes
entries.summary: The first-10-minutes path is the shortest local orientation path for running smoke checks and inspecting evidence.
entries.claim_boundary: The quickstart is local technical-preview proof, not package publication or production readiness.

entries.title: Python Wrapper Client
entries.summary: The Python wrapper client exposes local capability and smoke surfaces without adding external engine fallback dependencies.
entries.claim_boundary: The Python client is not a broad DataFrame or production SQL runtime claim.

entries.title: Source-Free Generated Output
entries.summary: Source-free generated output separates no-dataset smoke from user-generated rows and ShardLoom-native generator plans that can write local outputs.
entries.claim_boundary: Current source-free output is scoped to local smokes and does not imply broad SQL/DataFrame, object-store, or Foundry runtime.

entries.title: Dirty CSV Cleanup
entries.summary: Dirty CSV cleanup is a local fixture family for parsing, validation, clean/cast posture, and evidence boundaries.
entries.claim_boundary: Dirty CSV fixtures do not prove broad data-quality or production ETL runtime.

entries.title: Nested JSON Field Scan
entries.summary: Nested JSON field scan is a fixture family for extracting selected structured values while keeping materialization and claim boundaries visible.
entries.claim_boundary: Nested JSON fixture smoke does not imply universal document, media, or variant runtime support.

entries.title: CDC Overlay
entries.summary: CDC overlay is a local fixture pattern for applying small changes over a larger base while preserving explicit evidence boundaries.
entries.claim_boundary: CDC fixture smoke does not imply production incremental ETL, live, or hybrid runtime support.

entries.title: Result-Sink Proof
entries.summary: Result-sink proof records output write, replay, digest, and boundary evidence where supported by the scoped workflow.
entries.claim_boundary: Result-sink proof does not imply all output formats or table commits are production-supported.

entries.title: Output Fanout
entries.summary: Output fanout is the planned ability to reuse prepared state while writing multiple local output formats through explicit OutputPlans.
entries.claim_boundary: Fanout rows are workflow coverage evidence, not speed rankings or table/object-store commit claims.

entries.title: CSV Local Input
entries.summary: CSV local input is one of the local compatibility surfaces that can participate in certified local or fixture-smoke workflows.
entries.claim_boundary: CSV support is scoped and does not imply all adapters or object-store sources are supported.

entries.title: Parquet Local Input
entries.summary: Parquet local input is a local compatibility surface with explicit evidence and claim boundaries.
entries.claim_boundary: Parquet local evidence does not imply lakehouse table runtime or object-store support.

entries.title: JSONL Local Input
entries.summary: JSONL local input supports selected local fixture and compatibility workflows with explicit decode/materialization evidence.
entries.claim_boundary: JSONL fixture evidence does not imply broad document, media, or object-store runtime.

entries.title: Arrow IPC Boundary
entries.summary: Arrow IPC is an interoperability and compatibility boundary, not ShardLoom's internal execution substrate.
entries.claim_boundary: Arrow compatibility does not mean Arrow-native internal execution or external engine fallback.

entries.title: Avro/ORC Boundary
entries.summary: Avro and ORC are compatibility surfaces that require format-specific evidence before support can be claimed.
entries.claim_boundary: Avro/ORC rows must stay scoped or blocked until runtime evidence exists.

entries.title: Vortex Result Artifact
entries.summary: A Vortex result artifact is the highest-fidelity persistence target for ShardLoom-native outputs where a scoped path is admitted.
entries.claim_boundary: Vortex output claims are scoped to the specific output path and evidence refs.

entries.title: OutputPlan Reuse
entries.summary: OutputPlan reuse separates sink/write planning from input format so a prepared source can fan out to multiple local outputs without coupling input and output types.
entries.claim_boundary: OutputPlan reuse is a planned evidence contract and does not imply production sink, object-store write, or table commit support.

entries.title: Object-Store Boundary
entries.summary: The object-store boundary keeps S3, GCS, and ADLS parsing/planning separate from runtime reads, writes, credentials, and commits.
entries.claim_boundary: Object-store URI recognition or planning does not imply object-store runtime support.

entries.title: Table/Lakehouse Boundary
entries.summary: The table/lakehouse boundary separates metadata smoke, table scans, appends, deletes, commits, rollbacks, and object-store-backed runtime claims.
entries.claim_boundary: Metadata smoke does not imply table runtime or table commit support.

entries.title: Foundry Dev-Stack Smoke
entries.summary: Foundry dev-stack smoke is a local/style-only proof path that keeps real Foundry runtime and compute invocation disabled.
entries.claim_boundary: Foundry local proof is not Foundry production support, Marketplace proof, or Spark fallback.

entries.title: REST Control Plane
entries.summary: The REST control plane is a future API surface for capability, planning, lifecycle, and evidence, not a current HTTP listener.
entries.claim_boundary: REST contracts do not imply a production server or remote execution runtime.

entries.title: MCP Agent API
entries.summary: The MCP agent API is a future agent-facing control surface for safe discovery, validation, explanation, and evidence access.
entries.claim_boundary: Agent API posture does not authorize unrestricted execution or external effects.

entries.title: Package Channel Boundary
entries.summary: Package channel boundaries distinguish local package proof from GitHub pre-release, TestPyPI, PyPI, Homebrew, Scoop, winget, conda-forge, GHCR, and crates.io claims.
entries.claim_boundary: No package channel is ready without channel-specific install, smoke, provenance, and rollback proof.

entries.title: Universal Adapter Catalog
entries.summary: The universal adapter catalog classifies source, sink, catalog, object-store, relational, warehouse, API, SaaS, and media adapters by maturity and blockers.
entries.claim_boundary: Adapter visibility is not adapter runtime support.

entries.title: Evidence Level
entries.summary: Evidence level reports proof depth independently from execution mode: minimal runtime, certified, or full replay.
entries.claim_boundary: Evidence level does not silently upgrade claim status.

entries.title: Minimal Runtime Evidence Level
entries.summary: Minimal runtime omits heavy replay proof unless requested while preserving no-fallback and not-claim-grade posture.
entries.claim_boundary: Minimal runtime is not claim-grade unless a later scoped gate explicitly approves it.

entries.title: Certified Evidence Level
entries.summary: Certified evidence level emits normal execution, Native I/O, materialization, policy, and claim-gate evidence for a scoped local run.
entries.claim_boundary: Certified evidence is scoped and not a broad production or performance claim.

entries.title: Full Replay Evidence Level
entries.summary: Full replay adds result-sink replay proof to certified evidence for deeper output verification.
entries.claim_boundary: Full replay is stronger evidence, not a faster path or production sink claim.

entries.title: Fused Operator Pipeline
entries.summary: Fused operator pipeline evidence records when scoped prepared/native paths avoid intermediate full-table materialization across adjacent operators.
entries.claim_boundary: Fusion evidence is scoped and does not imply broad SQL/DataFrame or performance superiority.

entries.title: Compressed/Encoded Kernel Registry
entries.summary: The encoded kernel registry records encoding/operator admission, execution, decode, materialization, and claim blockers.
entries.claim_boundary: Kernel admission is not encoded-native support by itself.

entries.title: ShardLoom Session
entries.summary: ShardLoomSession is a scoped in-process session concept for reusing prepared artifacts, metadata, source state, and evidence recorder state across local batch rows.
entries.claim_boundary: The session is not a daemon, remote server, hidden global cache, or public Python runtime claim.

entries.title: Scale Profile
entries.summary: Scale profiles define bounded-memory, split, spill, shuffle, object-store, table, distributed, and Foundry scale evidence classes without claiming any-volume support.
entries.claim_boundary: Scale profiles are not Spark-displacement, distributed runtime, object-store runtime, table runtime, or any-volume claims.

entries.title: Package Publication Boundary
entries.summary: Package publication boundaries keep local install proof separate from public package release, tags, channels, and registry claims.
entries.claim_boundary: No public package release claim is allowed until publication gates pass with human approval.

entries.title: Release Readiness Gate
entries.summary: The release readiness gate checks security, provenance, architecture traceability, package proof, unsupported paths, and claim boundaries before release claims are allowed.
entries.claim_boundary: Readiness checks fail closed before any package upload or tag creation.

entries.title: Security/Governance Report
entries.summary: Security and governance reports track credentials, redaction, external effects, destructive operations, path safety, and no-fallback posture.
entries.claim_boundary: Security reporting does not authorize credential use, network calls, or production policy runtime.

entries.title: License And Provenance
entries.summary: License and provenance checks keep dependency licensing, SBOMs, checksums, attestations, and copied-code risks visible before publication.
entries.claim_boundary: Provenance dry runs are local proof only and create no package upload.

entries.title: Public Claim Boundary
entries.summary: Public claim boundaries map each user-visible claim to required correctness, benchmark, certificate, Native I/O, security, release, and no-fallback evidence.
entries.claim_boundary: Missing evidence keeps performance, production, Spark-displacement, SQL/DataFrame, object-store/lakehouse, Foundry, and package claims closed.
